question.text: SEBI mandates that listed companies must declare dividends in which format?

answer.text: As a percentage of the face value only
As a percentage of the book value per share
As a percentage of the market price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates that listed companies must declare dividends in rupees per share. This brings transparency and makes it easier for investors to understand the actual cash they will receive, rather than having to calculate from face value percentages. **The Trap:** Traditionally, companies declared dividends as a percentage of face value (e.g., 60% on FV Rs.2 = Rs.1.20 per share). While companies may still mention the percentage internally, the official declaration must be in rupees per share as per SEBI mandate. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) covers basic dividend concepts; NISM Series V-A covers dividend impact on mutual fund schemes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When explaining dividend announcements to clients, always refer to the per-share amount rather than percentage, as face values vary across companies and can mislead comparisons.
